title: How we shipped v0.4.0
excerpt: A retro on the supa.page typed-CRUD + drafts release — sync_files retired, rollback removed, SQLite as source of truth.
body:
Two-week sprint to flip supa.page off the file-based source tree and onto typed CRUD over SQLite. Here's what landed and what we cut.

## The shape change

Pre-v0.4.0, content lived in a `source/` directory on disk per site — `source/site.json`, `source/pages/*.json`, `source/posts/*.md`. Edits went through a `sync_files` MCP tool that batched file writes. It worked, but the file tree was a leaky abstraction: snapshot history forced us to keep N copies on disk per publish, the diff implementation re-read the whole tree, and we couldn't add row-level features (per-page A/B, per-post `published` flags backed by indexes) without inventing a sidecar database anyway.

v0.4.0 makes the database the source of truth. Pages, posts, and site_config are typed columns. The MCP surface grew typed CRUD per content type:

- `list_pages`, `get_page`, `upsert_page`, `delete_page`
- `list_posts`, `get_post`, `upsert_post`, `delete_post`
- `update_site_config`

Plus a `discard_changes` tool to throw away drafts.

## What got cut

- `sync_files` — replaced by typed CRUD.
- `rollback_site` + `list_publishes` + snapshot history — the right answer is to edit forward and re-publish.
- The on-disk `source/` tree — there's nothing to sync.

## What got better

- `<site>.supa.page` updates within ~100ms of `/publish` (vs ~2s of snapshot-copy before).
- The preview channel opens an SSE stream and live-reloads on draft writes.
- Diff is a single SQL query.
